reviewed_by_lane: product-rule-verifier using Google Developer Knowledge MCP for GCP and first-party Antigravity web retrieval where the MCP corpus lagged
policy: Repository review prefers Google Developer Knowledge MCP, then Context7 or official-web retrieval. Antigravity runtime verification is tool-neutral and requires the linked first-party page, not MCP. PASS requires exact support for the stated scope; GAP remains visible and blocks strategy-changing use. Prices are dated runtime inputs.

claims.claim: gcloud CLI credentials are distinct from Application Default Credentials
claims.review_note: Official documentation states that the gcloud CLI does not use ADC.

claims.claim: The bq CLI supports gcloud service-account impersonation
claims.review_note: Record configured impersonation without printing an access token.

claims.claim: gcloud CLI operations use the active configured account unless service-account impersonation is configured
claims.review_note: Inspect account/configuration only; never print an access token.

claims.claim: Standard edition maximum reservation size
claims.review_note: Re-check edition and location constraints at runtime.

claims.claim: Baseline and maximum reservation sizes normally use 50-slot increments when not covered by excess commitments
claims.review_note: Fluid scaling and excess-commitment behavior remain runtime checks.

claims.claim: Capacity is billed per second with a one-minute minimum by default; fluid scaling can change minimum-duration behavior when enabled
claims.review_note: Verify whether fluid scaling is enabled for the target.

claims.claim: Reservation predictability exposes Preview max_slots and scaling_mode fields separately from autoscale.max_slots
claims.review_note: Preview availability must be rechecked at runtime. Keep a fallback where these fields are unavailable; idle-sharing ceiling semantics are tracked separately.

claims.claim: A configured reservation limit is not an absolute consumption ceiling when shared idle slots are available, and the top-level idle-slot cap is best-effort
claims.review_note: Report ignore_idle_slots, historical slots_max_assigned, and configured limits as separate evidence.

claims.claim: Cached-result query jobs can have an empty reservation field without consuming reservation capacity or on-demand compute
claims.review_note: Report cached jobs separately from null-reservation compute jobs.

claims.claim: Cost-optimized Slot Recommender guidance covers supported Enterprise/Enterprise Plus reservation workloads and on-demand-to-Enterprise scenarios, not generic Standard sizing
claims.review_note: Generic INFORMATION_SCHEMA recommendations remain a separate evidence surface.

claims.claim: Storage Write API exactly-once semantics require application-created streams and correctly managed offsets
claims.review_note: Do not generalize to all Write API usage.

claims.claim: On-demand query price per TiB is location-, currency-, and date-dependent
claims.review_note: PASS covers the durable runtime-input rule, not a numeric price.

claims.claim: Storage Write API and legacy streaming prices and free tiers are location-, currency-, and date-dependent
claims.review_note: PASS covers the durable runtime-input rule, not a numeric price.

claims.claim: Interactive query queue limit is 1000 queued queries per project per region
claims.scope: interactive queries per project and region
claims.review_note: Re-check before reporting as OFFICIAL.

claims.claim: A dataset storage billing model change takes about 24 hours to apply and cannot be changed again for 14 days
claims.review_note: Report the lock-in warning without emitting DDL.

claims.claim: CAPACITY_COMMITMENTS_BY_PROJECT is the near-real-time current commitment inventory
claims.scope: reservation administration project and exact location
claims.review_note: Query 0.2a keeps current rows separate from change events.

claims.claim: CAPACITY_COMMITMENT_CHANGES_BY_PROJECT is a change-event view whose deleted commitment records are retained for at most 41 days
claims.scope: reservation administration project and exact location
claims.review_note: Raw events are not deltas to sum and cannot establish unqualified historical coverage.

claims.claim: Historical reservation comparisons use time-aligned RESERVATIONS_TIMELINE fields; current reservation configuration alone can be inaccurate for past usage
claims.scope: reservation administration project and exact location
claims.review_note: Queries 0.3 and 0.4 use current RESERVATIONS metadata only as current context, not as the historical denominator for the declared analysis window.

claims.claim: total_bytes_billed is on-demand billing evidence but can be informational or unavailable; normalized_literals identifies successful non-cache GoogleSQL query shapes
claims.value: qualified billed bytes and privacy-safe query hash
claims.review_note: Query 4.2 restricts billed-byte evidence to null-reservation, non-cache jobs and surfaces missing-byte gaps; failed jobs use hashed diagnostic handles instead of query hashes.

claims.claim: JOBS_BY_PROJECT retains 180 days of project-scoped regional job metadata and requires project-wide job visibility
claims.scope: workload project and exact BigQuery location
claims.review_note: Missing jobs.listAll blocks or limits project-wide JOBS_BY_PROJECT evidence.

claims.claim: JOBS_TIMELINE_BY_PROJECT retains 180 days of project-scoped regional job timeslices and requires project-wide job visibility
claims.scope: workload project and exact BigQuery location
claims.review_note: Missing jobs.listAll blocks or limits project-wide JOBS_TIMELINE_BY_PROJECT evidence.

claims.claim: Standard edition has no baseline slots and does not support configurable target job concurrency
claims.review_note: Do not propose a Standard baseline or target concurrency.

claims.claim: Standard edition supports project-level QUERY and PIPELINE assignments but not folder- or organization-level assignments
claims.review_note: This cookbook analyzes and recommends QUERY assignments only; PIPELINE is documented but outside its capacity metrics.

claims.claim: Standard edition does not support slot commitments
claims.review_note: Do not propose a commitment for Standard capacity.

claims.claim: A workload with an applicable reservation assignment does not automatically spill excess demand to on-demand billing
claims.review_note: Excess assigned demand can queue, wait for idle capacity, or use configured autoscaling; null reservation evidence is not overflow evidence.

claims.claim: Reservation autoscaling charges for scaled slots rather than measured slots used by jobs
claims.review_note: JOBS slot usage is not an autoscaling invoice; use Billing evidence for economic comparison.

claims.claim: WRITE_API_TIMELINE metadata covers Storage Write API ingestion and does not establish legacy tabledata.insertAll usage
claims.review_note: Do not infer legacy streaming presence or absence from Query 5.1.

claims.claim: Antigravity discovers workspace skills under the active workspace root at .agents/skills/<skill-folder>/SKILL.md
claims.review_note: Launch agy from bq_finops_cookbook for discovery.

claims.claim: Gemini 3.5 Flash is available as an Antigravity reasoning model; current public model documentation does not establish a separate Flash thinking-level control
claims.review_note: Do not block execution on undocumented thinking=High.

claims.claim: Antigravity CLI exposes /model, /skills, /mcp, /permissions, and /tasks controls; MCP configuration is optional for this cookbook
claims.review_note: Verified by retrieving the current first-party CLI reference through official web because the Google Developer Knowledge corpus did not expose this newer route. The skill uses /model, /skills, and /permissions; /mcp is not a prerequisite.

claims.claim: Antigravity permissions enforce tool policy independently of behavioral prompt guardrails
claims.review_note: Verified through current first-party Antigravity web retrieval. Deny takes precedence over Ask and Allow. Resolve current permission targets from the official page instead of a finite cookbook prefix list.

claims.claim: /agents monitors subagents and /tasks monitors active background shell processes
claims.review_note: Verified through current first-party Antigravity web retrieval. The main agent selects subagent tools and permissions; reviewers receive no write or cloud-mutation access. No platform-enforced further-delegation switch is claimed unless the runtime exposes one.
